prompt:
Add Restart=on-failure to xdg portal services so the user session can recover from D-Bus disruptions without manual intervention.

## Context — what happened to motivate this

On classic-laddie 2026-05-05 08:31:24 EDT, dbus-broker hit its per-UID byte quota for UID 1000 and force-disconnected several user-session D-Bus consumers. Among them: `xdg-desktop-portal.service` (exited and stayed inactive) and `xdg-document-portal.service` (exited with status 20, ended up in failed state). Neither service auto-restarted because the upstream nixpkgs unit definitions don't include Restart=on-failure.

Downstream effects from those two never coming back:
- waybar exited 2 minutes later (couldn't reach the portal)
- wireplumber's session graph went stale (lost track of ALSA cards), so the Focusrite USB audio interface became invisible to PipeWire even though the kernel still saw it
- The session limped along for 4 days until the user noticed missing UI elements

The proximate cause (dbus quota) is its own conversation; this PR addresses the *durability* problem — that one transient blip permanently broke services that should self-heal.

## Change to make

Add a small NixOS module (or extend an existing one) that adds Restart policy to the user-session portal services. In nixpkgs these services come from the `xdg-desktop-portal` package; cosmo can override their serviceConfig via `systemd.user.services.<name>.serviceConfig`.

Services to cover (read /etc/systemd/user/ on classic-laddie or grep nixpkgs to confirm exact names if any are missing):
- `xdg-desktop-portal`
- `xdg-document-portal`
- `xdg-desktop-portal-gtk`
- `xdg-desktop-portal-hyprland`
- `xdg-permission-store` (worth covering for the same reason)

For each, set:
```nix
Restart = lib.mkDefault "on-failure";
RestartSec = lib.mkDefault "5s";
StartLimitBurst = lib.mkDefault 5;
StartLimitIntervalSec = lib.mkDefault 60;
```

The `StartLimit\*` knobs prevent infinite restart loops if a service is genuinely broken (e.g., misconfiguration). After 5 failures in 60 seconds, systemd gives up — at which point manual intervention is warranted anyway.

Use `lib.mkDefault` so per-host overrides remain possible.

## Where to put this
Investigate the existing repo structure first:
- `modules/common/desktop.nix` is the natural home if it's already imported by hosts that have a desktop session
- If desktop.nix is imported by all hosts (some headless), then create `modules/common/desktop-resilience.nix` (or similar — match cosmo's idiom) and have desktop hosts import it
- Note that PR #529 (currently open) is doing a similar split for memory protection. Coordinate naming so this fix doesn't fight #529's structure. If #529 has already moved interactive-session protection to a new module, drop these portal restart configs into the same module — same scope, same import set.

Read the current state of #529's branch (`agent/20260509-1008-38fc`) to see what landed there, then make a coherent decision.

## Investigation step before editing
- `grep -rn 'xdg-desktop-portal\|xdg-document-portal' modules/ hosts/` to see if cosmo already configures these
- Read whatever module already touches portals (probably nothing, but worth confirming)
- Check #529's branch state for any new modules that this fix should live alongside

## CRITICAL: no local nix tooling
- No `nixos-rebuild`, no `nix build` (even `--dry-run`), no `nix flake check`, no `nix eval`.
- PR #529 hasn't landed yet, so the OS-level memory protection that would make local validation safe isn't in effect on the running system. Building locally still risks wedging classic-laddie. CI does the dry-run validation on a runner.

## PR description requirements
- The 2026-05-05 incident (one-paragraph summary): dbus quota → portals dropped → no Restart policy → 4 days of degraded session
- Why `StartLimit*` knobs are needed (prevent restart loops on genuine breakage)
- Note that this is a durability fix, not a fix for the proximate cause (the dbus quota event itself is a separate concern, not addressed here)
- Note coordination with PR #529 if the new module structure overlaps
- Reference the cosmo session experience: 'durable healing of transient failures' is a recurring theme

## Acceptance
- Restart=on-failure + RestartSec + StartLimit* applied to all xdg-desktop-portal-related user services
- Configured via `lib.mkDefault` for per-host override
- Lives in a desktop-scoped module (not in everything-everywhere common)
- PR description covers incident + rationale
- CI green
